description: §6.8.1 E-DERIVED-WRITE — `default=` on a `const <name>` derived declaration. `const <doubled default=0> = @count * 2` is rejected: `default=`/`reset()` on a derived cell is a write, forbidden for the same reason as direct assignment. Incidental W-PROGRAM-* ignored by superset.
rationale: §6.8.1: `default=` on a `const` derived declaration is E-DERIVED-WRITE — assigning/resetting a derived cell is always a write error. The plain (no-default) derived declaration is legal; only the `default=` form fires.
